title: Symphyotrichum peteroanum
taxonomy: Symphyotrichum peteroanum is classified within the family Asteraceae. This plant is a species of flowering plant. uses: Symphyotrichum peteroanum is also known as New England Aster, and it has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's leaves and flowers are used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. New England Aster is also believed to have antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin infections. intro: Symphyotrichum petiolatum, also known as Peter's aster, is a perennial plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). Peter's aster has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and combating infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Peter's aster is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support immune function and alleviate respiratory issues.
